name: polars
tagline: Reference guide for the Polars Python DataFrame library: install, expressions, lazy evaluation, I/O, joins, and pandas migration.
verdict: Clean, benign reference skill. No credential access, network egress, shell-piped execution, destructive operations, or hidden/manipulative instructions found. Only risk is that install/example commands (uv pip install, python snippets) exceed the declared allowed-tools: Read scope, and there's no guidance on install/query failure handling or when NOT to use

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use for X' guidance (e.g. small in-memory scripts where pandas suffices) — a user can't tell from the description when polars is the wrong choice.
Frontmatter declares `allowed-tools: Read` (line 5) yet the body instructs running shell installs (line 22, 27) and executing Python code — tool scope and instructed actions are inconsistent.
No guidance on handling install failures, version mismatches, or runtime errors (e.g. schema mismatches on join, missing columns) — failure paths are unaddressed.
